Free calculator · 90 seconds

Fractional CMO ROI calculator.

Adjust the inputs to your situation. The calculator compares year-one all-in cost and likely outcome across three options: fractional CMO, full-time CMO hire, and founder-led DIY. Calibrated to 2026 market benchmarks.

Your inputs
Affects the realistic comp range and ROI expectations
Marketing strategy, campaign approvals, agency oversight, content review — anything marketing-related
12 hrs
Founder salary + dilution + opportunity cost — typical founders: $250–$500/hr
$350/hr
Typical: 20–40 hours/month. Lower for advisory, higher for embedded operator.
25 hrs
Year-one comparison
—
Fractional CMO
$120K
Year-one all-in
Monthly fee$10K
Setup & onboarding—
Equity dilutionNone
Recruiter feeNone
Notice period30 days
Hours/month25 hrs
—
Full-time CMO
$520K
Year-one all-in
Base + variable$375K
Benefits$45K
Equity (1–2% est. value)$50K+
Recruiter fee$75K
Notice period3–9 mo
Hours/month~160 hrs
—
CEO-led (DIY)
$218K
CEO opportunity cost
CEO hours/year on marketing624 hrs
CEO hourly cost$350/hr
Equity dilutionNone
Recruiter feeNone
Notice periodn/a
Strategic expertiseLimited

Recommended for your stage: Fractional CMO

For early growth companies ($2M–$10M), the fractional model captures the senior strategic value of a CMO without the dilution, recruiter risk, or 9-month onboarding ramp. Switch to full-time when you cross $25M–$30M and the function genuinely requires 40+ hours/week of leadership.

How the math works

What's behind the calculation

Fractional cost = monthly fee × 12. The default monthly fee scales with the requested hours/month (using $400/hr blended rate as a benchmark).

Full-time cost includes base + variable + benefits + equity-value + recruiter fee, scaled to your stage. Equity value is estimated at the midpoint of typical CMO grants (1–2% of company value at next round).

DIY (CEO-led) cost = (CEO hrs/week × 52) × CEO hourly cost. This is the opportunity cost of CEO time spent on marketing rather than on CEO-level work. Most CEOs significantly underestimate this number.

For the deeper analysis of when each fits, see fractional CMO vs. full-time CMO and signs you need a fractional CMO.
